label: 28 to 43 Repealed. , title XIII, § 1313,July 1, 1944, ch. 37358 Stat. 714
notes: Section 28, acts , ; , ; , ch. XV, § 3, , provided divisions under Assistant Surgeons General and the rank, pay, and allowances of said Assistants. See sections 206, 207, and 210 of this title.July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 332 Stat. 712Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309July 9, 1918, ch. 14340 Stat. 886
Section 28a, , , provided for a chief of the narcotics division. See .act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 10(c)46 Stat. 152section 206 of this title

Section 29, acts , ; , , provided for conferences with State and Territorial boards of health. See .July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 732 Stat. 713Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 242n of this title

Section 30, acts , ; , , provided for compilation of mortality, morbidity, and vital statistics. See .July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 832 Stat. 714Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 137 Stat. 309section 242k(g) of this title

Section 31, , , provided that officers of Service could make allotments of their pay. See , Pay and Allowances of the Uniformed Services.act June 5, 1920, ch. 235, § 141 Stat. 883section 704 of Title 37
Section 32, , , provided for purchase of quartermaster supplies by officers of Service. See .act Mar. 6, 1920, ch. 94, § 141 Stat. 507section 210 of this title
Section 33, , , provided for limitations on expenditure of appropriations. See .act Mar. 4, 1921, ch. 161, § 141 Stat. 1378section 227 of this title
Section 33a, , , provided for covering into Treasury moneys collected for treatment of foreign seamen and other pay patients. See .act May 14, 1935, ch. 11049 Stat. 229section 221 of this title

Section 34, acts , ; , ; , , provided for titles for officers of the Service. See .July 1, 1902, ch. 1370, § 132 Stat. 712Aug. 14, 1912, ch. 288, § 237 Stat. 309Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 10(a)46 Stat. 152section 207 of this title
Section 35, , , provided titles for officers other than medical officers of Service. See .act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 10(a)46 Stat. 152section 207 of this title
Section 36, , , provided titles for officers in grade of Assistant Surgeons General. See .act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 10(a)46 Stat. 152section 206 of this title

Section 37, acts , ; , , provided for promotions, pay and allowances, and severance from Service of commissioned officers. See sections 209 et seq. of this title.Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 946 Stat. 151Nov. 11, 1943, ch. 298, § 757 Stat. 588
Section 38, , , provided for appointment and grades of medical, dental, sanitary engineer, and pharmacist officers. See .act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 446 Stat. 150section 209 of this title

Section 39, , , provided for number, pay and allowances, and service credits for pay purposes of medical, dental, sanitary engineer, and pharmacist officers. See sections 209 et seq. of this title.Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 546 Stat. 150
Section 40, , , provided for appointment and qualifications of employees other than commissioned officers. See .act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 1146 Stat. 152section 209 of this title
Section 41, , , provided for appointment of persons other than commissioned officers for scientific research. See .act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 746 Stat. 151section 209 of this title
Section 42, , , provided for medical and hospital services to officers disabled by sickness or injury. See sections 213 and 248 of this title.act Apr. 9, 1930, ch. 125, § 1246 Stat. 152

Section 43, , , related to transfer of property and equipment to Service. , , formerly cited to section 43, was repealed by , .act Mar. 3, 1919, ch. 98, § 340 Stat. 1303Act Mar. 4, 1921, ch. 15641 Stat. 1365act Aug. 10, 1956, ch. 1041, § 5370A Stat. 641
